items.body: The Travelers Championship closes out the PGA Tour Signature Event calendar at TPC River Highlands in Cromwell, Connecticut, with Keegan Bradley back to defend after a dramatic birdie on the 72nd hole last year. Scottie Scheffler enters as the clear betting favorite off another U.S. Open top-five and a win here in 2024, while Tommy Fleetwood, Xander Schauffele, and Cameron Young headline a 72-player field that includes nearly every eligible top-50 player except Rory McIlroy. River Highlands is a classic Pete Dye par-70 that typically rewards accurate driving and aggressive iron play, so expect low scores and a fast leaderboard if the Connecticut weather cooperates.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+400 to +480):
items.body: The World No. 1 and betting favorite who won the 2024 Travelers in a playoff and has three top-10s in his last three starts at TPC River Highlands.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+1600 to +1900):
items.body: Last year's runner-up and a polished ball striker whose all-around game fits a course where precision off the tee matters.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+1475 to +1800):
items.body: The 2022 champion and one of the Tour's steadiest big-event performers when his putter is cooperating.

items.label: Cameron Young (+1700 to +2200):
items.body: A power player in strong 2026 form who can overpower River Highlands when his driver is dialed in.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+1800 to +2000):
items.body: An elite young ball striker whose tee-to-green consistency keeps him in contention at scoring-friendly setups.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+2000 to +2200):
items.body: A precision iron player whose control game travels well on a compact par-70 where mistakes get punished.

items.label: Sam Burns (+2000 to +2200):
items.body: Fresh off a U.S. Open runner-up finish and dangerous when his putter heats up on a course that often produces low numbers.

items.label: Russell Henley (+3300 to +4000):
items.body: Tied for second last year and a reliable Connecticut performer whose accuracy suits River Highlands.

items.label: Keegan Bradley (+4500):
items.body: The defending champion and two-time Travelers winner with deep local knowledge as the lone New England native in the mix.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: TPC River Highlands in Cromwell, Connecticut.

items.label: Established:
items.body: 1952; Travelers has been the title sponsor for 20 consecutive years, making it one of the Tour's longest-running corporate partnerships.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Keegan Bradley won the 2025 edition at 15-under 265, edging Tommy Fleetwood and Russell Henley by one shot with a birdie on the final hole.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: Connecticut's annual PGA Tour stop and a Signature Event since 2024, with a strong charity footprint and one of the Tour's most enthusiastic galleries.

items.label: Course:
items.body: TPC River Highlands, Cromwell, Connecticut.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: June 25 through June 28, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $20 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 72-player Signature Event field, 72-hole stroke play.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: Par 70, 6,844 yards; a Pete Dye design known for scorable par-5s, demanding par-4s, and a closing stretch that often produces late drama.

items.label: Thursday:
items.body: 3-6 p.m. ET on Golf Channel.

items.label: Friday:
items.body: 3-6 p.m. ET on Golf Channel.

items.label: Saturday:
items.body: 1-3 p.m. ET on Golf Channel; 3-6 p.m. ET on NBC.

items.label: Sunday:
items.body: 2-4 p.m. ET on Golf Channel; 4-7 p.m. ET on NBC.

items.label: Streaming:
items.body: PGA Tour Live on ESPN+ all week; NBC simulcast on Peacock.
